name: hig-technologies
description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines for technology integrations including Siri, Apple Pay, HealthKit, HomeKit, ARKit, and more
capabilities: Provide HIG-compliant implementation checklists for 28 Apple technologies
Generate privacy and permission requirement documentation
Create user flow diagrams from permission prompts to task completion
Identify required versus optional features for App Store approval
Supply testing guidance including edge cases for each technology
Cross-reference related HIG topics like inputs, components, and status indicators
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains only markdown documentation files referencing Apple's Human Interface Guidelines. All 2880 static analysis findings are false positives - they are pattern matches in documentation text (URLs to Apple developer docs, code examples in markdown blocks, technical terminology) rather than executable code. No scripts, network calls, or system access present. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Developers struggle to implement Apple technologies correctly while meeting App Store guidelines. This skill provides authoritative HIG guidance for Siri, Apple Pay, HealthKit, and 25+ Apple frameworks to ensure approval-ready implementations.
userTitle: Apply Apple HIG standards to technology integrations
limitations: Provides guidelines only - does not generate working code implementations
Cannot verify current API changes beyond documented HIG versions
Does not replace official Apple developer documentation or WWDC sessions
Focuses on iOS/macOS/watchOS/tvOS - limited web implementation guidance

useCases.title: Siri Integration Planning
useCases.description: Define Intents, App Shortcuts, and voice interaction patterns that meet Apple's conversational design standards

useCases.targetUser: E-commerce app developer adding payment options
useCases.title: Apple Pay Checkout Implementation
useCases.description: Implement payment flows with correct button styles, authentication, and security requirements for App Store approval

useCases.title: HealthKit Permission Design
useCases.description: Create privacy-compliant health data access patterns with proper usage descriptions and user consent flows

promptTemplates.title: Basic Technology Guidelines
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the Apple HIG requirements for integrating [technology name] into my app? List the key principles and must-follow guidelines.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with a new Apple technology

promptTemplates.title: Implementation Checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a step-by-step implementation checklist for [specific feature] following Apple HIG. Include required vs optional features and common rejection reasons.

promptTemplates.title: Privacy and Permissions
promptTemplates.prompt: What privacy requirements and permission prompts do I need for [technology]? Provide example usage description strings and explain what data access requires user consent.

promptTemplates.title: App Store Review Preparation
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my [technology] implementation plan against HIG guidelines. Identify any patterns that could cause App Store rejection and suggest corrections.

bestPractices: Always check for .claude/apple-design-context.md before asking questions - use existing context first
Request only the minimum data permissions needed and explain the user benefit before each permission prompt
Test all accessibility requirements including VoiceOver labels, Dynamic Type, and Switch Control compatibility
antiPatterns: Do not create custom payment button designs - Apple Pay buttons must use Apple-provided APIs
Never request health data without explaining the specific health benefit to the user first
Avoid AR experiences that feel gimmicky - only use when spatial context provides genuine value

faq.question: Which Apple technologies does this skill cover?
faq.answer: 28 technologies including Siri, Apple Pay, HealthKit, HomeKit, ARKit, Core ML, Generative AI, iCloud, Sign in with Apple, SharePlay, CarPlay, Game Center, In-App Purchase, NFC, Wallet, VoiceOver, Maps, Mac Catalyst, and more.

faq.question: Does this skill generate working code?
faq.answer: No - it provides HIG guidelines, implementation checklists, and design patterns. Use it alongside coding skills to implement the recommended patterns.

faq.question: How do I know if my App Store submission will be approved?
faq.answer: This skill identifies HIG compliance requirements and common rejection reasons. Final approval depends on Apple's review team and current guidelines.

faq.question: Can this help with web implementations of Apple technologies?
faq.answer: Partially. Apple Pay on Web and Sign in with Apple have web-specific guidance included, but the focus is native iOS/macOS/watchOS/tvOS development.

faq.question: Does this cover the latest iOS version requirements?
faq.answer: The skill references HIG documentation which is updated by Apple. Always verify current requirements in official Apple Developer Documentation for the latest iOS version.

faq.question: What should I do before using this skill?
faq.answer: Check for .claude/apple-design-context.md in your project. This file may contain additional context that reduces the need for follow-up questions.
